citation_density.finding: Section 5.5 (Recursion) has been re-grounded against the newly added 4th resource (realpython.com/python-recursion/, role=supporting). It now carries 6 occurrences of [4]: the recursion definition, the base-case definition, the recursive-case definition, the factorial(n)/0!=1 definition, the call-stack definition, and the RecursionError/'maximum recursion depth exceeded' claim are all cited. The previously-flagged hard miss (an entire uncited sub-concept with no covering resource at all) no longer applies -- resource [4]'s own notes field explicitly lists 'base case, recursive case, factorial example, call stack, RecursionError / max recursion depth,' matching every claim now marked [4]. Two illustrative passages remain uncited by design and are treated as author-derived worked-example/synthesis rather than resource claims: (a) the step-by-step arithmetic trace of factorial(4) unwinding (1 -> 1*1 -> 2*1 -> 3*2 -> 4*6), which is a mechanical derivation from the code shown immediately above it, not an external fact; (b) the closing comparison to the Topic 2.2 for-loop equivalent ('Recursion is not faster here...'), which is author synthesis/framing, not a standalone factual assertion. Neither is a miss under the rubric. Section 5.5 standalone-claim coverage is now ~6/8 distinct factual assertions cited (~75%), comfortably above the 60% floor and no longer a structural (zero-coverage) miss.
citation_density.section_5_overall_coverage_estimate: ~19/22 standalone factual assertions across 5.1-5.6 carry a citation marker (~86%), well above the 60% strict-mode floor.
other_uncited_spots_still_present: 5.2: 'There is one ordering rule to remember: parameters with defaults must come after parameters without them...' -- still no citation marker. Minor; does not affect the 60% floor and is not the class of miss (entire uncited sub-concept with no covering resource) that strict mode hard-fails on.
5.1: 'A function with no return statement (or a bare return) hands back the special value None.' -- still no citation marker. Same minor-gap category.

5.4: 'A variable defined at the top level of your file... functions can read it. But if a function assigns to a name, Python treats that name as local by default...' -- still no citation marker. Same minor-gap category.

sequence_drift.detail: Re-verified against content/curriculum/sequence.index.json id=3.1 concepts_introduced (function definition and calling; return values; positional/keyword/default arguments; *args and **kwargs; local vs global scope; recursion (base case + recursive case); docstrings). Every technical term used in the corpus maps onto this list. No terms from 3.2 (Functional Constructs: lambda, map/filter, comprehensions) or later topics appear. The recursion-section revision added no new concepts -- only citation markers and existing prose -- so no new drift was introduced by the fix.

non_blocking_findings: topic_corpus.md front matter still declares 'resource_count: 3' even though topic.resources.json now lists 4 resources (the new realpython.com/python-recursion/ entry). This is a metadata-sync issue, not one of the four gating checks, so it does not affect the pass/fail verdict here -- but it should be corrected before this corpus is treated as final.
verdict: PASS - all four checks pass on re-check. The targeted revise added proper [4] citation markers to section 5.5 (Recursion) tied to the newly-added 4th resource (realpython.com/python-recursion/, role=supporting), which fully covers recursion/base-case/recursive-case/factorial/call-stack/RecursionError per its own notes field in topic.resources.json. All 4 captured resources (1 primary, 3 supporting) are now cited at least once, no fabricated citation markers exist, section 5 standalone-claim coverage is ~86% (well above the 60% strict-mode floor for resources_source=web_search), required sections 3/4/5/10 are all present and non-trivial, no forward-referenced terms exist against 3.1's own concepts_introduced or any prior topic in sequence.index.json, and word count remains within the deep profile's 1800-2200 range per .claude/config/artifact-mix.json. Two minor pre-existing uncited spots remain (5.2 parameter-ordering rule, 5.1 None-default-return fact, 5.4 global-scope-assignment rule) but these are isolated sentence-level gaps, not an uncovered sub-concept, and do not pull aggregate coverage below the strict-mode floor -- flagged as non-blocking for future polish. Also flagging a non-blocking metadata drift: topic_corpus.md front matter says 'resource_count: 3' but topic.resources.json now has 4 entries; recommend syncing before final sign-off.
